name: Discover
tagline: Understand the problem and decide what to build.
canon: No solution may proceed without a named customer, a written problem, and evidence the problem is worth solving.

editor.lead: Discovery is where AI has changed the most, and the fastest — and where it’s easiest to fool yourself.
editor.body: The cost of synthesis has collapsed. Eighty interviews become six personas in an afternoon; a week of competitive reading becomes a morning. But the failure mode moved with the cost — the bottleneck is no longer gathering, it’s trust.
points: **Make every model claim traceable to a source** — a transcript timecode, a quote, a link. Untraceable synthesis is worse than none.
**Keep a human naming the problem.** Agents are fluent at restating solutions as problems; that’s the one thing Discover can’t outsource.
**Treat speed as runway, not the finish line.** Faster discovery should buy more cycles, not fewer questions.

activities.name: Customer research
activities.canon: Talk to a named customer before writing a line of spec.
activities.editor: The highest-leverage place to point an agent in the whole lifecycle.

activities.name: Problem framing
activities.canon: State the problem as a problem, not as a disguised solution.

activities.name: Requirements & specs
activities.canon: Requirements trace to evidence. Open questions are listed, not hidden.

name: Build
tagline: Design, prototype, and ship the thing.
canon: Every step produces a reviewable artifact. Decisions are recorded with their rationale. Quality gates precede release.

editor.lead: The protocol asks for reviewable artifacts and recorded decisions. Agents happen to be unreasonably good at exactly that.
editor.body: Drafting the spec, decomposing the plan, reviewing the PR, writing the eval — these are the load-bearing chores of Build, and they’re where teams see the most consistent leverage. The ones getting durable value share a few habits.
points: **The agent proposes; a human commits.** Keep agents non-destructive — comment, draft, queue — never auto-merge or auto-close.
**Record the decision and the reason, not just the diff.** The rationale is the artifact future-you actually needs.
**Gate quality before release.** A 90-second eval beats a post-incident postmortem every time.

activities.name: Design & prototyping
activities.canon: Show a working artifact, not a description of one.

activities.name: Code review & QA
activities.canon: A second reviewer sees every change before it ships.
activities.editor: Calibrate the agent to comment, never to approve.

activities.name: Technical decisions
activities.canon: Record the decision and the reason. Future-you is the audience.

activities.name: Testing & evals
activities.canon: Regressions are caught before release, not after.

name: Grow
tagline: Launch, learn, and keep it running.
canon: Releases are measured and reversible. Operations have an owner. Findings return to Discover.

editor.lead: Growth and operations are where the durable wins live, because the work recurs.
editor.body: A triage skill that saves fifty hours a week compounds every week; a one-off discovery win doesn’t. The risk here is the inverse of Discover’s — not too little rigor, but too much trust in an unattended loop.
points: **Measure, and make it reversible.** Every rollout stage should be one you can undo.
**Keep a human in the path for anything irreversible.** Unattended automation is fine for proposing, dangerous for deciding.
**Close the loop back to Discover** — today’s operational findings are next quarter’s problems.

activities.name: Launch & GTM
activities.canon: Roll out in stages. Every stage is reversible.

activities.name: Rollout & enablement
activities.canon: Someone owns getting people to actually use it.

activities.name: Iteration & optimization
activities.canon: Findings return to Discover as new problems.

activities.name: Operations & toil
activities.canon: Recurring work has an owner and a runbook.
activities.editor: The compounding-returns activity. If you’re new, start here.
